subject: DevOps
title: Jenkins: The Pioneer
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Jenkins: The Pioneer.

hook.text: Jenkins was the first popular CI server. Born in 2011 as a fork of Hudson, it dominated for a decade.

buildup.text: Jenkins is open source, self-hosted, and endlessly extensible. Over 1800 plugins cover every use case.

discovery.text: Jenkinsfile brought pipeline-as-code to Jenkins. Groovy scripts define stages, steps, and conditions.

twist.text: Jenkins' age shows. Managing plugins, updates, and security is a full-time job. Newer tools are simpler.

climax.text: Still, Jenkins runs millions of pipelines worldwide. Legacy systems rely on it heavily. It's far from dead.

punchline.text: Jenkins pioneered CI/CD. Complex but powerful and still alive.

quiz.question: What is a common challenge with maintaining Jenkins?
options: Managing plugins, updates, and security patches is complex
It cannot run on Linux servers
